intended_use: Fetches read-only content from a fixed allowlist of public APIs (currently api.github.com, api.wikipedia.org), summarises findings, and writes a report to the per-run sandbox workspace. May delegate file_read/openai_call operations to a child data-analyst agent.
out_of_scope_uses: POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH operations against any endpoint without explicit human approval
Fetching content from any FQDN outside the capability-manifest allowlist
Calling internal or private network ranges (SSRF guard enforced)
Acting on behalf of an end user without an authenticated signed identity envelope
known_limitations: Retrieved web content is rescanned by Azure AI Content Safety Prompt Shields; an indirect prompt injection that evades both Shields and the regex layer could still influence downstream reasoning
Egress allowlist is static — changes require redeploy
Token budget capped at 100,000 per run; very large pages may be truncated

nist_ai_rmf_map.govern: GV-1.1, GV-1.2, GV-4.3 — manifest + signed envelope + delegation policy
nist_ai_rmf_map.map: MP-2.3, MP-3.4, MP-5.1 — intended-use scoping + risk classification + egress allowlisting
nist_ai_rmf_map.measure: MS-1.1, MS-2.5, MS-2.7, MS-3.2 — anomaly scoring + retrieved-content rescans + audit telemetry
nist_ai_rmf_map.manage: MG-1.3, MG-2.4, MG-4.1 — kill switches + human-in-the-loop approvals + DSAR/incident response

evaluation_summary: Pre-release: passed sandbox suite, policy denial suite, gateway header validation, run-authorization tests, prompt-injection regex + Prompt Shields fail-closed tests. Review cadence: quarterly.
